title: zornia pratensis subsp. pratensis
intro: Zornia pratensis subsp. pratensis, also known as Wild Madder, is a flowering plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as wound healing and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Zornia pratensis subsp. pratensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various forms of use in traditional medicine and herbalism.
